---
title: "Quest 18: Wrappers - Add Behavior Around Functions"
subtitle: "Decorator-Like Patterns in Rust"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
🎁 QUEST 18 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 10 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/18-decorators.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/18-decorators.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Rust does not have Python decorators directly, but we can wrap behavior.
This gives you a clean way to add repeated setup/teardown logic.

## 💡 Wrapper Pattern

```rust
fn with_logging<F>(name: &str, action: F)
where
    F: Fn(),
{
    println!("[start] {}", name);
    action();
    println!("[end] {}", name);
}

fn main() {
    with_logging("greet", || {
        println!("Hello from wrapped function");
    });
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Wrap a function that prints a study reminder and include start/end messages.
Pick your own label and message to personalize it.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

```rust
fn with_logging<F>(name: &str, action: F)
where
    F: Fn(),
{
    println!("[start] {}", name);
    action();
    println!("[end] {}", name);
}

fn main() {
    with_logging("reminder", || {
        println!("Take a break, then continue coding.");
    });
}
```

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 18 Complete
You learned a practical Rust wrapper pattern.
Next: [Quest 19: Structs and Impl](19-classes.qmd)
:::
